name: TravelGuide — Generate major airport pages
description: Weekly cloud agent that generates Markdown guide pages for the world's busiest airports not yet covered. Opens a PR when new pages are created.

workflow.prompt:
You maintain TravelGuide in this repository — one practical airport guide per airport, stored in Postgres (DATABASE_URL is available in the environment).

Each run:
1. Read lib/major-airports.ts for the ranked list of busiest airports (ACI 2024).
2. Run `pnpm guide list` to see which IATA codes already have guides.
3. Pick the next batch of missing airports starting from the highest rank (up to 10 per run).
4. For each missing airport, run: pnpm generate:airport <IATA>
   (AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is available in the environment. This validates and writes the guide straight to the database.)
5. Review each new guide with `pnpm guide show <IATA>` for placeholder URLs or empty sections. To fix issues: export with `pnpm guide show <IATA> /tmp/<iata>.md`, edit (e.g. full country names, not ISO codes), then `pnpm guide save /tmp/<iata>.md`.
6. Do not overwrite existing guides unless they are clearly broken.

Quality gate:
- Preserve the heading structure used by existing guides (see `pnpm guide show LHR`).
- Use real official airport URLs in frontmatter sources.
- Skip generation if AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY is missing — report that instead.

Guides go live via database writes — no pull request needed for content. Reply with a status note listing each airport added (rank, IATA, name).
If all top-100 major airports already have guides, reply with a short status note.
